senses.definition: the most anterior point on the upper jaw between the two central upper incisors, used in craniometry and dental anatomy
examples: The prosthion is a standard landmark used in cranial measurements.
In classical craniometry, researchers locate the prosthion on the maxilla.

senses.definition: relating to or involving the prosthion or the front part of the upper jaw
examples: Prosthionic measurements were recorded for the study.
The prosthionic point is identified in the skull analysis.

senses.definition: the dental specialty focused on restoring and replacing missing teeth and related oral structures with artificial devices
examples: Prosthodontia is the branch of dentistry concerned with dentures and implants.
She studied prosthodontia to learn about crowns and bridges.

senses.definition: plural form referring to the dental specialty of prosthodontia
examples: Various prosthodontias are available in modern clinics.
The curriculum covers multiple prosthodontias and related procedures.

senses.definition: relating to the branch of dentistry that restores and replaces teeth and related structures
examples: She pursued prosthodontic training after dental school.
Prosthodontic techniques include crowns and implants.

senses.definition: the dental specialty dealing with replacement and restoration of teeth and surrounding tissues
examples: He specializes in prosthodontics and restorative procedures.
Prosthodontics often involves dentures, crowns, and implants.

senses.definition: a dentist who specializes in prosthodontics
examples: She is a renowned prosthodontist in the city.
A prosthodontist designs and fits dentures and implants.

senses.definition: plural of prosthodontist
examples: The conference invited several prosthodontists.
Prosthodontists collaborate with surgeons on complex reconstructions.

senses.definition: slang for a prostitute, a sex worker; a person who sells sexual services
examples: The street corner had posters advertising prosties.
He spoke with caution about the prostie who offered services nearby.

senses.definition: plural slang term for prostitutes
examples: The alley had several prosties offering services.
Propaganda posters warned about prosties in the area.

senses.definition: a medication or drug name historically used to treat certain conditions, though usage may be outdated or context-specific
examples: The doctor prescribed prostigmin in the older trial.
Prostigmin was once used for neuromuscular disorders.

senses.definition: a person who engages in sexual activity for money or other compensation
examples: The town had a history of prostitution.
Prostitutes worked in the red-light district.

senses.definition: to engage in sexual activity with someone in exchange for money
examples: The man was arrested for prostituting himself.
Some people choose to prostitute their talents online.

senses.definition: sexually exploited or engaged in prostitution for money in the past
examples: She was prostituted as a teen in the red-light district.
They prostituted themselves to survive in the city.

senses.definition: in a way that is like prostitution; in a manner that is sexually exploitative or for money
examples: The marketplace was prostitutely crowded with bargains.
He spoke prostitutely, swapping favors for money.

senses.definition: plural of prostitute; people who engage in prostitution
examples: The area was known for its many prostitutes.
Support services help prostitutes seek other work.

senses.definition: the act of engaging in prostitution or offering to engage in sexual services for money
examples: She was arresting people for prostitution.
The policy targets those who are prostituting themselves online.

senses.definition: the practice or occupation of engaging in sexual activity in exchange for money or other compensation
examples: Prostitution is illegal in some countries.
The city is tackling issues related to prostitution and trafficking.

senses.definition: the act of engaging in a sexual act for money as a business or activity
examples: Prostitution can involve various forms of sex work.
Her research examined the economics of prostitution.

senses.definition: plural form of prostitution; multiple instances or cases of prostitution
examples: The report details various prostitution cases.
Policies aim to reduce prostitution and trafficking.

senses.definition: an old or rare term for someone who engages in prostitution or a broker in prostitution activities
examples: Historical texts mention a prostitutor aiding the trade.
The term is rarely used in modern English.

senses.definition: plural of prostitutor; historical or rare term for persons involved in prostitution networks
examples: Ancient texts refer to prostitutors as organizers of illicit markets.
The word prostitutors appears in archival law manuscripts.

senses.definition: a plural or variant form of a Greek-root term; in English dictionaries it is not a common standalone word
examples: The term prostoa appears in some anatomical glossaries.
This form is rarely used in modern English texts.

senses.definition: a medical term for an artificial opening made into a body part, such as the mouth or another organ
examples: Prostomia is created during certain surgical procedures.
Care for prostomia includes stoma management.

senses.definition: relating to a prostoma or to the mouth opening created surgically
examples: Prostomial care is essential after stoma surgery.
Users with prostomial devices require specialized equipment.

senses.definition: to form or create a prostoma or opening surgically
examples: The surgeon will prostomiate to relieve intestinal blockage.
Prostomiation is a complex procedure requiring careful planning.

senses.definition: in anatomy, the anterior part of an invertebrate head segment; in craniology it may refer to a preoral lobe or lip area
examples: The prostomium is the first segment of certain annelids.
In some anatomical diagrams, the prostomium is labeled at the front.
